concepts.name: Agent
concepts.definition: A governed digital actor that can observe context, reason over intent, use approved capabilities, and produce accountable outcomes.

concepts.name: Agent Identity
concepts.definition: The persistent identity of an agent across systems, tenants, runtimes, and lifecycle states.

concepts.name: Agent Role
concepts.definition: The function an agent performs inside a system.

concepts.name: Agent Capability
concepts.definition: A declared ability that an agent may use when permitted by policy and context.

concepts.name: Agent Runtime
concepts.definition: The execution environment where an agent loop, tool call, workflow, or skill runs.

concepts.name: Agent Control
concepts.definition: The layer that constrains agent action through objective, policy, approvals, budget, risk, and kill-switch rules.

concepts.name: Agent Governance
concepts.definition: The accountability model that defines ownership, responsibility, auditability, trust, risk, and separation of duty.

concepts.name: Agent Evidence
concepts.definition: Recorded proof of agent input, decision boundary, approval, action, result, and verification.

concepts.name: Agent Conformance
concepts.definition: The validation process proving that an implementation follows the canonical concept, schema, policy, and runtime boundary.

concepts.name: Reconciliation
concepts.definition: The process of comparing desired state and observed state, then safely reducing drift.
